title: senna goniodes
intro: Senna goniodes, also known as the African senna or Cassia goniodes, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family. The plant is a perennial shrub with yellow flowers and is native to tropical regions of Africa. Senna goniodes has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a laxative due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. Herbal preparations of Senna goniodes are often made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
